Dialog.name: MND_000_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}I answered correctly...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}Everyone in the class is[n]looking at me with respect...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MND_001_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}I answered incorrectly...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_002_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15205 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's enough about mammoths... I don't see the big deal. They're just huge elephants.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, that wraps it up for the Stone Age. *sigh* I'm tired of these lessons about fossils.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'd rather talk about the Sengoku era! Japanese history's not exciting enough until then.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But, it's what I'm paid to do, so here we are... It's not easy being an adult.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_003_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Next is... the Jomon period? When do the samurai get their day in the sun!?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15216 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'll just ask a question at random and call it a day. Hmm... {f 4 1}-kun.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_004_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The places where people dumped their waste in the Jomon period—what are they called nowadays?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_006_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15217 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's right, they're called "middens." The word is Scandinavian in origin and means "dung hill."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...Eh, that didn't work. Still not interested in this at all.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_007_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15219 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}*sigh* It's no use teaching you guys if you're going to get the simplest questions wrong.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The correct answer is "midden." The word is Scandinavian in origin and means "dung hill."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, I suppose that isn't important here, ultimately.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_008_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ugh, I wanna get to the age of katanas! Everything else is so dull...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_009_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16014 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}*sigh* I don't really feel like teaching today...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Last night, I was watching TV when I saw a special on trains.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Traveling by train is always nice, isn't it? Staying in a sleeper car is a traveler's dream.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Speaking of trains, let's talk about the rhombus-shaped instrument on the roof of the train.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_010_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Power is generated when this device comes into contact with the overhead wires...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16016 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You look like you know what I'm talking about, {f 4 1}. Let's see if that's the case.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_011_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 5 21 0 100}What do you call the device that helps generate electric power for the train?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_013_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16017 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's right. You're better informed than I thought. Or perhaps you're simply a railfan?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_015_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Most modern trains take in electricity by bringing the pantograph into contact with the overhead wires.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}However, the monorail we use has the pantograph hidden under the train instead...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_016_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15602 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's discuss the Earth's rotation today.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Earth completes one full rotation every 24 hours...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_017_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}All right, don't fall asleep on me now. This may be basic, but it's an important premise for the rest of the lesson.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_018_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Do you know Léon Foucault? He's a French physicist who performed experiments regarding the rotation of the Earth.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which tool did he use in his experiments?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_020_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15617 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Yes, a pendulum. The device he built has become eponymized as the Foucault pendulum, with full-scale replicas installed around the world.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_021_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15619 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}No, it was a pendulum. The device he built has become eponymized as the Foucault pendulum, with full-scale replicas installed around the world.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_022_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Flip to the next page— Oh, this is an application. Let's skip that and... Hmm, that's an experiment. Okay, um...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_023_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16206 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's look at the paragraph on page 30, shall we?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"Kate was feeling a bit depressed today, but Jessie invited her to tea."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Has everyone been feeling all right lately? Considering the season we're in now...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_024_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Yes, "May sickness" is a real phenomenon. We've made it through the brunt of it, but it may still be lingering![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_025_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What's the other name for "May sickness"—the more casual one?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_027_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's believed that the sudden change in schedule at one's new office or school brings about stress and a depressive mood. Thus, we get the May blues.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_028_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's believed that the sudden change in schedule at one's new office or school brings about stress and a depressive mood. Thus, we get the May blues.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_029_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That'll be on the exam, so make sure you write it down![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We've still got a couple minutes left... Let's talk about my experience studying in India.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_030_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Don't you hate when you know the meaning of a word, but you can't remember the word itself?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...This feels like a good time for a spontaneous question.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_031_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's see... Iori's shifting in his seat a bit too much, so instead...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_032_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which phrase means, "able to see things for what they really are"?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_034_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15814 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's correct! You may very well have a keen eye yourself.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_035_0_00
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15816 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Bzzt, nope! If you don't develop a keen eye for the truth, you'll find yourself easily tricked.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_036_0_00
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Having a keen eye means you're able to see things for what they really are. It can also help you make good decisions for your future.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That goes for the rest of you, too—you need to learn how to look past the surface. Otherwise, you'll grow up to be superficial.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_037_0_00
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We all need to start somewhere. Rome wasn't built in a day![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The same goes for magic. You ought to learn its background.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Today, I'll teach you about the origin of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Long, long ago, at the dawn of civilization, man created primitive religions.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Animism, totemism, and shamanism all fall into this category. You learned these in world history, right?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That last one, shamanism, is important to the origins of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A shaman would fall into a trance state and act as the intermediary between the worlds, but...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It counts as "magic," where humans can have the power of gods and spirits in their own hands.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This shaman-style magical tradition would later be embraced by witches.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Take the common European witch, as an example.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}People today think that witches derive their power from contracts with the devil...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But that's just Christian propaganda.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The witches' predecessors were more like maidens serving the gods.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It was through prayer to those gods that they earned their powers.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They learned to control their magic without losing consciousness during their trance states.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's how they originally awoke to the practice.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Originally, Europe was full of religions, worshiping a slew of various gods.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The collective term for them is "paganism."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Interestingly enough, the celebrations of Christmas and Halloween have pagan backgrounds.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_038_0_00
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you all still paying attention? No trance states for now! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a test to see if you were paying attention.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong, and your afterlife won't be pleasant. "Palas Aron Azinomas"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_039_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What early religious practice was the origin of magic?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_041_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Very good... You'll be born in your next life as a human.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_042_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! Enjoy your next life as a chupacabra.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_043_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, paganism and Christianity have a closer connection than one might think.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As the Roman Empire grew, so did the Christian population within the empire.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At first, each religion practiced separately without issue, but...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_044_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14802 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Please sit down so we can begin today's lecture. "Ada Ada Io Ada Dia"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I know you were expecting Mrs. Terauchi, but she's had an emergency. I'll be taking over class today.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We're going to learn a bit about natural magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's not too dissimilar to paganism, which we've already discussed.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Natural magic is, obviously enough, magic that draws its power from nature.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The most popular form of this would probably be herbalism.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Calendar-based spells are also a form of natural magic, since they involve the seasons.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, some of you may already be familiar with dowsing, which uses a pendulum or rod to find things underground.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This too is a form of natural magic, first used to detect sources of water.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's enough examples for now. Let's move on to the proper mindset for natural magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}First, you must recognize yourself as a part of nature and accept its power.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In other words, think of humanity as a microscopic player within the cosmology of the universe.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This mindset is based on the concept of the four classical elements.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ancient magicians believed that different objects sharing a common element would react to one another.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They performed countless experiments attempting to prove their hypotheses about nature.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_045_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you all listening? I certainly hope so! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a little test to see if you were paying attention...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You'll receive an ill omen if you get it wrong. "Ada Ada Io Ada Dia"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14815 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, who to pick today...? {f 4 1}! You have been called upon![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_046_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What form of natural magic is used to find water sources?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_048_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14818 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Excellent work—your answer is correct. As for your omen, no news is good news.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_049_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14809 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'm afraid that's not it. Watch out for the letter that arrives at midnight...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_050_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But, back to the main subject—prayer is considered integral to many forms of natural magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}One characteristic of natural magic prayer is that much of it is in praise of the earth goddess.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This ties into the concept of natural magic as a way of invoking nature's blessing.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As time passed, natural magic developed into natural science.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In this transition, though, a key element was lost...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The notion of "time" being controllable.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Natural magic forbids the use of spells that go against the standard flow of time.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Its practitioners believe that magic should not defy nature, and to this day, take great care to follow natural law.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_051_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15808 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's see here... {f 0 1 2}Finals start next Tuesday{f 0 1 27}. Geez, we just had an exam! What a pain...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, maybe "pain" isn't the right word... Some phrases can be pretty tricky.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, let's talk about comma splices.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_052_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'm sure you've all heard of them before, but just to be clear...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A comma splice is when two independent clauses are incorrectly joined by a comma instead of a conjunction.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_053_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15845 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a question for you all! Dun dun dunnn... {f 4 1}-kun![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Where is the comma splice found in the following sentence?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"Because it is 10:30 right now, we will not reach the theater in time, it's already started, to my dismay."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_055_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15842 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ah! Correct! The middle one is the inaccurately-used comma.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_056_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15816 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Bzzzzt! The inaccurately-used comma is between "time" and "it's."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_057_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Regardless of how logical the sentence flow may appear...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A comma cannot be used to link two independent clauses.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 2 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...Well, if no one has any questions, let's move on.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_058_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15202 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's talk about the Heian era today. I won't spend a lot of time on this, so pay attention.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In 794, the Heian-Kyo was established. In 894, they abolished imperial embassies to China...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I might put this section on the exam next week... I'm feeling lazy.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_059_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, on to feudalism. Listen carefully, because this has to do with the origins of the samurai![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_060_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, the law permitting permanent ownership of newly-cultivated land served as a foundation for the development of feudalism.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Thanks to this decree, a lot of land was cultivated...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At the same time, a serious problem arose for the farmers. Do you remember what that was?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_063_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}C'mon, you can do better than that! You're descended from the samurai![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_064_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The work doesn't end after you plow the land. It costs a lot of money to build waterways and stuff on top of that.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, the farmers with more wealth and authority could make more money by expanding their lands...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}On the other hand, those who weren't as affluent had no choice but to remain poor—it's the same old story throughout time.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, that's all for the Heian period.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_065_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14802 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Silence! Class is beginning. "Uriel, Rilue, Ilili, Eulir, Leiru"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Just a quick preface: when I use the word "magic," I mean it in a very general sense.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, today's topic is the magic of medieval Europe.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'll be giving a crash course on the single most magical place and time in recorded history.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, there are a lot of materials pertaining to Western magic, from printed books to communal folklore.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'll cover all of it, including the changes European magic has seen since then.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As I mentioned previously, European magic finds its origins in paganism.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Starting with Christianity, though, it has been influenced by an influx of various cultures.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's see, what are some well-known examples...?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There's Kabbalah, which is the esoteric study of mystical aspects of Jewish texts.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There's also Gnosticism, a Christian sect founded by Simon Magus, the Sorcerer.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Greece's philosophy and natural sciences were the fruits of magical researchers...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And the same was true of Islam's scientific advances, which surpassed Europe's at the time.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, as for the circumstances of magic in this age...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It was an era where the rule of Christianity and the insight of the natural sciences collided.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Magicians bravely reached up for a connection with the gods...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_066_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Say, are you still awake? You're not daydreaming, right? Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How about a test to see if you were paying attention?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You'll know what type of guardian angel you have, depending on how you answer. "Uriel, Rilue, Ilili, Eulir, Leiru"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_067_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What is the esoteric study of Jewish texts called?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_069_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Very good. Thank your guardian angel, because they must be kind.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_070_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! You've got a fallen samurai from Okehazama for a guardian angel.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_071_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The magicians of the age achieved great things...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Particularly the renowned "grimoires."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A grimoire is a collection of magical texts, usually as all-encompassing as possible, wherein...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_072_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15203 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here we go! Finally, we've reached Taira no Masakado! He's the turning point of the era of the samurai![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_073_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It all began with infighting among the Taira clan. Their conflict escalated until the Imperial Court had to intervene.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Masakado was pursued by the Imperial Court, so he had no choice but to openly oppose them...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The result was known as the War of Masakado.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15216 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}{f 4 1}! You may not look like a samurai, but let's see if you know this one anyway.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_074_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There's a theory that Taira no Masakado thought up the prototype for an item essential to battle.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, what do you think that item was? It's something every samurai needs![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_076_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15218 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Knew that one already, huh? Yep, it's the katana![n]{f 1 1}

{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Think of samurai and their swords are the first thing that come to mind! Although, it's not known whether Masakado really came up with them.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_077_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15209 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...No, it's the katana. Seems pretty obvious why, doesn't it?[n]{f 1 1}

{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Think of samurai and their swords are the first thing that come to mind! Although, it's not known whether Masakado really came up with them.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_078_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This is going to be on the {f 0 1 2}exam next week{f 0 1 27}, so prepare well![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's skip forward a little to the Kamakura era.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ah, at last! I can feel them on their way... The samurai are almost here...![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In 1185, the Kamakura shogunate was established. Yes! We're there![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1083 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And of course, its founder was Minamoto no Yoritomo... I can't believe we finally made it![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_079_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15620 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}All right, quiet down! Summer vacation's over! You're back in school now, so act accordingly![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We'll never get through the full textbook if you stay in vacation mode forever.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_081_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which property of electricity is relevant to superconductivity?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_083_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anything that reaches an electrical resistance of zero at a characteristic temperature is considered superconductive.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_084_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Superconductors are materials capable, at the right temperature, of reaching zero electrical resistance. Resistance is correct![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_085_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As a result, electric currents can travel eternally through a superconductive circuit held at the proper temperature.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Many years ago, I used this fact to confess my love to someone...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"My love for you is superconductive!" I told them.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Sure enough, my enthusiasm won her over, and we started dating...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But enthusiasm isn't very scientific. Before too long, I'd lost interest in her.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1084 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}All right, in the next section...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_086_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you ready? Then, let's begin. Today, we'll discuss the magic of the Renaissance era.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Renaissance was a time when the arts and the sciences enjoyed a strong resurgence in Europe.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Of course, this had a profound influence on magic...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Although it may be more accurate to say the two studies helped improve one another.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As to why the Renaissance helped to further magic...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Practitioners had finally nailed down a way to escape the rule of the church.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}By studying the teachings of previously-inaccessible sects, knowledge of magic flourished, possibly like never before.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Renaissance also brought about the revival of ancient Greek and Roman literature.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Magical texts, such as the Hermetica, received all-new translations and increased study.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Philosophy, natural history, alchemy, astrology...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This book is filled with a wide range of subjects. Some even consider it the bible of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Just as science and astronomy have made strides in the modern day...[n]{f 1 1}

{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You can see this in the emergence of one of the great magicians of all history—one who put the knowledge of the Renaissance into application.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}John Dee was a professor who advised the Queen of England, but...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}With the magical knowledge he uncovered, he was able to communicate with angels.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Of course, there was also Hohenheim, a doctor who traveled across Europe...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}He became known as Paracelsus, a renowned physician and alchemist.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Granted, the magician's life wasn't an easy one back then...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Both men dealt with great amounts of persecution in their lives.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_087_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you still listening? Even your astral selves need to pay attention! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a test to see if you were paying attention.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and your house will become permanently haunted. "Bagahi Laca Bachabe"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_088_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which book helped further the art of magic during the Renaissance?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_090_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Correct. Enjoy sleeping in your peaceful home tonight.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_091_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! Now you'll see a woman's face in the ceiling of your room...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_092_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}During the Renaissance, magic had a surprisingly strong influence on the church-ruled society.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Several magicians were even involved in the core part of the Renaissance—the religious revolution...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_093_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16203 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We're starting a new chapter today. This one is all about Nick's tough situation.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You know, the story reminds me of when I first met my husband. What a wild time that was![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_094_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At first, we were somewhat distant, but after spending enough time together...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}One day, I suddenly realized that he was my better half.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_095_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16221 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ooh, I just came up with a good question! Mr. {f 4 1}, since our eyes met first...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Who is one referring to when speaking of their "better half"?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_097_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16218 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Yes, wonderful! Your "better half" is considered your soulmate—the person you are destined to be with![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_098_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16219 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}No, no, no. Your "better half" is considered your soulmate—the person you are destined to be with![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_099_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Someone who you never want to leave, and will treasure always...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I believe whoever makes you feel this way is your soulmate.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Someday, if ever you meet someone who makes you feel like that...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Accept your feelings—and your better half—with open arms.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...Wow, look at the time! Let's get back to the lesson.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_100_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14802 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Take your seats and we'll begin. "Hi-fu-mi-yo-i-mu-na-ya-ko-to-mo-chi-ro-ra-ne"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This is... math class normally, yes?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, Mr. Miyahara is feeling under the weather, so I'll be filling in for him.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Since you came expecting a math lesson, I'll be talking about numerology.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Numerology is a type of magic which sees the world through a system of numbers.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Essentially, its practitioners convert matter into numbers.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Numbers have power, so numerologists merely take advantage of that power.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some say the first true numerologist was Pythagoras.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You may know him from the Pythagorean Theorem, but he's also quite famous for practicing magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}He and his pupils researched what is now called Pythagoreanism.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They realized that everything existing in this world corresponds to a number.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They considered odd numbers masculine, and even numbers feminine.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They also worked towards verifying the existence of holy and unholy numbers.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Pythagoreans focused on the numbers 1, 2, 3, and 4, because they add up to 10, which represents completion.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They considered those first four numbers to be holy numbers, the most important of all.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They're known as the Tetractys.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Later, others would build upon the numerological foundation that the Pythagoreans laid.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Even recently, studies have been performed on the relationship between names and numbers.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's look at this a little closer, shall we?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let me teach you a method to interpret your own name's meaning.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Be sure to include your middle name, too![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, convert each letter of your name into a number—1 for A, 2 for B, and so on—then add the numbers together.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}If a letter has a two-digit number, like 14 for N, first add the two digits together to make a single digit.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Keep adding these digits together until you're down to a final number between 1 and 9.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Upon learning or reflecting on that digit's properties, you can conclude the characteristics of the person in question.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Basically, your number represents your general personality.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_101_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Say, are you still listening? I hope all this talk of numbers hasn't put you to sleep! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a test to see if you were paying attention...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and the numbers will haunt you, even in your sleep! "Hi-fu-mi-yo-i-mu-na-ya-ko-to-mo-chi-ro-ra-ne"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_102_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What is the collective name for the four holy numbers in numerology?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_104_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14818 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's correct. The actuarial tables predict a happy future for you...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_105_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Alas, no. Unfortunately, your sales tax at the school store will now increase by 25%.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_106_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A bit more on numerology and names before you're dismissed...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Numerology can also be used to discern your inner self, your public persona, your future, and so on.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Convert the vowels in your name into numbers, then add them up. That reveals your inner self.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Next, do the consonants in your name to get the persona you display in public.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Using this same method on your birth date will calculate your future...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But make sure you use Christian Era dates in this calculation.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's also possible to assess your compatibility with another person using numerology.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}By adding the digits of your birth date to the other's, you can...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_107_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16003 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}{f 0 1 2}Exams start next Tuesday{f 0 1 27}, but I'd rather spend the time doing experiments.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The precise moment when two chemical agents come into contact... It's so exciting![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'm sure you all find experiments more exciting than just sitting in a chair, listening to me talk, right?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_108_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, complaining about it won't do anything for us. All right, here's a question to get you motivated.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_109_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16016 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How about you, {f 4 1}? Your turn to answer my question today.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which hormone is the one in your brain that provides a sense of achievement?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_0112_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16020 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Haha, teaching wouldn't be any fun if students knew everything! The answer is "dopamine."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_113_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Dopamine's an important hormone that gives us a sense of accomplishment, joy, and excitement, which drives to motivate us.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}However, if it's overproduced repeatedly, that can lead to addiction.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's the same as other chemicals used in experiments, and many things in life. Moderation is key—make sure you remember that.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_114_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14803 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Okay, time to get started. Exams are coming up...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ready for today's lesson? "Ahah Adonai Tetragrammaton"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We've covered the time of magical growth and just about reached the modern age.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And just as every art or science branches off into sub-studies as it progresses...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Magic began subdividing around the 18th century.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some people preached of morals, some focused on communicating with spirits, etcetera.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There was even the man who founded modern psychology based on his research into hypnosis.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This, too, is magic: the ability to see into the human mind.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As a result of magical research's flourishing, witch hunts grew more common...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}To avoid this, the practice went "underground" to secret societies.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'm sure many of you have heard the names of at least some of these organizations.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Rosicrucian Order, the Freemasons, the Illuminati, the Golden Dawn...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Interestingly enough, many of these societies are still with us today.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As for the forefathers of modern magic, they are simply too numerous to count.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}For instance, there's Éliphas Lévi, a man who attempted to combine modern and ancient systems of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}His literature, which took a scientific approach, is still being published to this day.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Madam Blavatsky was a renowned magician of the 19th century and the figurehead of Theosophy in her lifetime.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Many magical societies stemmed from the Theosophical Society, which Blavatsky founded with a few close associates.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Franz Mesmer opened the path to psychology in his studies on life energy...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And Rudolf Steiner, once a member of the Theosophical Society, founded anthroposophy, which he called a "spiritual science."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There was Aleister Crowley, of course, the 20th century's most well-known magician...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And George Gurdjieff, a mystic who taught his many students the Fourth Way, also known as "The Work" or "The System."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Each one of them was a living archive of esoteric knowledge.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_115_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you still hearing me? Not frozen with fear, I trust? Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Allow me to test if you were paying attention.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and your sign will change. "Ahah Adonai Tetragrammaton"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_116_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Who was the founder of Theosophy, a group that gave rise to many other magical societies?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_118_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Very good. Remember to still get blessed on your unlucky years, though.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_119_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! Your sign is now the cat—your year will never come![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_120_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At any rate, the magical arts progressed even further with their practical use.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Thanks to the archiving and proliferation of these arts by the people we've covered today...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_121_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Hang on, what? Okay, uh, your assignment is to figure this one out.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_122_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, wasn't that fun? Numbers are so interesting![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Even with a difficult problem like this one, once it's solved, it becomes such a simple concept.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Our entire reality is made up of numbers. Civilization owes its very existence to math![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15415 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}All right, {f 4 1}-kun, you seem like a math fan—here's a question for you.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_123_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We used the number "zero" a lot. Do you know where it originally came from, though?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_125_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15417 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's right! You really must be a math fan![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_126_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15419 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Too bad! The correct answer is "India." I guess you aren't really that big a math fan, huh?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_127_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Isn't it fascinating how someone just up and conceived the concept of emptiness one day?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Thus, with the birth of the zero, all of mathematics took an enormous leap forward.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1085 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ahhh... The beauty and wonder of it all! Math is MAGNIFICENT![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_128_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16203 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...Just compare the names used for months in Japanese and English—the latter is a mess! October is tenth? Come on now![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, everyone knows about Julius Caesar, right? Well, it's not just his fault that the English names for months are off...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_129_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Did you know that his name is actually the origin of the word July?[n]{f 1 1}

{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Many people and gods have had their names used this way throughout history. Isn't it interesting to see how a person can affect entire languages?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_130_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which Roman goddess has been theorized as the namesake for the month of April?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_132_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some have speculated that April was originally called "Aphrilis," named after Aphrodite, the Greek name for the goddess Venus.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_133_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16220 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Aw, too bad! That was a tough one, though. The answer is "Venus."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some have speculated that April was originally called "Aphrilis," named after Aphrodite, the Greek name for the goddess Venus.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_134_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Others believe it may be derived from a Latin verb meaning "to open."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They argue that April is the beginning of the season where flowers and other plants begin to open up.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1583 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I, however, prefer my word origins to have a bit more of a romantic flair! Here's another example...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_135_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15830 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}*yawn* I stayed up a bit too late last night.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I didn't expect Mrs. Terauchi to pull such a legendary move in mahjong last night... I've never had that bad of luck before![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_136_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Is it because of the month? Ugh... I still can't get over it![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_137_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In most of Japan, October is considered "the month without gods" because they're not around...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But there's one region that instead calls it "the month with gods," because that's where they all go! Do you know which one it is?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_141_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}All of the gods in Japan gather each year in Izumo to meet and forge relationships with one another.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I want to be blessed with a loving relationship too... But first, I need some money, and then to find love, and then to work hard...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So I guess the first thing is, I need to go to Naganaki Shrine to pay my respects... Oh! It's already too late. Shoot![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_142_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16001 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The next chapter is about enzymes. Worried it'll go over your head?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, have no fear. You're already familiar with the main places enzymes can be found—your own body.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_144_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a question about sweet potatoes.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A certain enzyme brings out the sweetness in them when they're baked. Do you happen to know what it is?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_146_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16017 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Yep, that's it. Eat a lot of sweet potatoes, do you?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_148_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}When the beta-amylase in sweet potatoes breaks down the gelatinized starch...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It becomes a malt sugar called maltose. That's why heating sweet potatoes up makes them even sweeter.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The temperature at which beta-amylase is activated is said to be roughly 60 to 70 degrees Celsius...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which is why a slowly-baked potato is sweeter than one that was rapidly heated, like with steam.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_149_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Today, we'll be examining regional forms of magic...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}First up is India, one of the birthplaces of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You may not think of India as an especially magical place, but...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Consider how esoteric Buddhist magic came from India...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It was a definite influence on Gnosticism, and a foundation of modern-day theosophy.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's even said that the Upanishads, part of the Vedas, are the original books of magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Many ancient Indians were passionate in their search for magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}These Indian magicians emerged in similar circumstances as other places around the world.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In India, it's traditional to lead a pious life...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But eventually, some people claimed to have gained immense power through Tapasya.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At first, their claims were rejected, but they slowly gained power and numbers...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Eventually, they were accepted into common practice.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}These are the ascetics mentioned in the Upanishads.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}However, any normal human wouldn't be readily able to mimic their skills.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Those known as the Tapasa have surpassed the limitations of humanity.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Do you think you could fast and pray for months at a time, without eating or sleeping?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You'd die before attaining any powers at all![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}So, it's understandable that the Buddha rejected such extreme asceticism.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Of course, the Upanishads aren't all about such radical practices.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They contain very practical methods, too. Yoga, for instance, was also introduced through these scriptures.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_150_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Say, are you all listening? No out-of-body projections, now! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How about a test to see if you were paying attention?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and you'd better be ready for a heaping dose of bad karma. "Om Vajra Ratna Om"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_151_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What is the ancient Indian magical text I mentioned today?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_153_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Correct! You still need to live an honest life, though—I can't pile on any good karma.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_154_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! You get a whole year's worth of the principal's karma![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_155_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Moving on... There are many different schools of yoga, and near-countless asanas, or poses, being taught.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some asanas are for health, while others are for prayer.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}One important branch is Hatha yoga, a type of yoga that many people are already familiar with today.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In this branch, one cultivates a healthy body to attain purity of mind...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_156_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15004 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's November already... The third-year students must be cramming frantically by this point.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Don't worry, though—you'll share their fate next year. I can just see you now, tearing your hair out...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, it's a bit cold today, but there's no need for the heater. Let me tell you why...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_157_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Winter is supposed to be cold—you have to learn to appreciate the temperature, rather than curse it.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}That's the Japanese way. Do you understand me? You don't, do you?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As Sei Shonagon once wrote in her famous Pillow Book, "Winter, early mornings."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_158_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What was she describing with such a sparse line?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_161_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15009 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Have you been paying any attention? Just return from a trip to dreamland, maybe?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_162_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The author was describing her favorite time in winter. She's now famous for having written about her likes, dislikes, and other observations.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}She admires what the snow represents: the beauty of the winter frost.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Rather than curse the winter chill, she appreciated its positive qualities.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Oh, how wonderful Japanese culture is! Despite its modern youth, anyway...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And if you think my hair's frosty, remember that you'll eventually be just like me![n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_163_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15002 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Oh, what a sublime heroine Murasaki-no-Ue is![n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}She was written to be the ideal woman of the Heian period—the quintessential Heian beauty.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_164_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15013 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Speaking of Murasaki-no-Ue, here's a question for you, {f 4 1}...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_165_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In the Tale of Genji, Murasaki-no-Ue is portrayed as a woman of the highest class in terms of appearance, intelligence, and character.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In the poem that Genji, the protagonist, sends to her, what did he compare her to?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_169_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"It means that the image of you, as beautiful as the wild cherry blossom, will never leave my mind..."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1082 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How refined! Compared to them, you guys are nothing but...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_170_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It's almost the end of the year—let's not ruin our last month this year with laziness, okay?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Oh, that's right—{f 0 1 2}exams start next Monday{f 0 1 27}! Though, didn't we just have them not too long ago...?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The material on the exam begins on page 160, and will include today's lesson as well.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_171_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's see... How about we go over the definition of allotropy?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"The property of certain elements that can exist in two or more forms that are chemically identical, yet physically discrete."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Does that make sense? Well, allotropy is very common—more common than you might think! Like oxygen, for example.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_172_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What's the name of the stratospheric layer of molecules comprised of three oxygen atoms?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_174_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 16018 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wow, good job! I'm sure you'll do fine on the exam.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_176_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The oxygen molecule is vital to humanity in general, but the ozone allotrope in particular is necessary for life on earth.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You are aware that the ozone layer absorbs harmful ultraviolet radiation, right?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Sadly, due to the destruction of our climate, there's less and less ozone protecting us...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I probably have nothing to worry about, but you kids might.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1582 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}We need to be more environmentally friendly for the sake of all life on this planet.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_177_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Today, I'll finally talk about our own country's magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}While you may think you know enough about it already, you probably don't know the half of it.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The belief that magic is a questionable subject is becoming more common...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But what is swept into the corners of society today was once central to it.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It could even be said that Japan came to be in the first place thanks to magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}After all, Himiko of Yama-taikoku was a shaman.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}She reportedly relied upon a type of shamanism called "kido" when ruling her domain.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Sadly, we have no way of knowing how her magic worked...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The same is true for Prince Shotoku, who established a centralized government.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}He practiced Onmyodo, so he was also a shaman of sorts.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}With origins like these, magic was naturally woven into the very fabric of Japan.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In the Heian period, for example...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The Bureau of Onmyo was established so that onmyoji could expel evil spirits, give spiritual advice, and train new practitioners.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}You may be familiar with it from the legend of Abe no Seimei.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Even in the age of the samurai, magicians had their part to play.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There were many generals who studied divination alongside military tactics—they could be considered magicians.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_178_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Say, are you still listening? Don't become mesmerized, now! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Here's a test to see if you were actually paying attention.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and I'll make some of your lingo taboo. "Tohokamiemitame"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_179_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What sorcery was Himiko said to have used?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_181_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14818 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Very good. Thank your kotodama powers for that one.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_183_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The ease of access to magic that Japanese citizens had continued up to the Edo period...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But the new government formed during the Meiji Reformation stamped it out.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Tsuchimikado Shinto, which had only recently been revived, faded away once more...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_184_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ms. Ounishi left early today, so I'll be filling in for her.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I think today we'll learn about near-death experiences.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There's no real reason for it—they've just been on my mind lately.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, a near-death experience is defined as something an individual undergoes just before death.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Is it true insight into the afterlife, or simply a trick of the brain?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Opinions vary, but it is certainly true that near-death experiences have been a hotly-debated topic for some time.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There are actually a few surviving records of ancient near-death experiences.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}According to the writings of Plato, a resuscitated soldier experienced the afterlife as a spirit.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}A similar incident was reported in Japan by ethnologist Kunio Yanagita in the Tono Monogatari.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Psychologist Carl Jung wrote that, in his own near-death experience, he saw a vision of the world from afar.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I'm sure you've heard your own stories, whether by famous or ordinary individuals.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I have a few firsthand reports with me today. Let me read you some of them.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"I felt as if my soul was detached from my body." "I met a friend who had already passed away."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"I saw my whole life flashing before my eyes..."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"I saw the most brilliant light." "I was bathed in pure light."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"My body was brimming with happiness." This feeling of pure joy is also known as euphoria.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}At any rate, these kinds of reports are typical for the experience.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some people have actually attempted to make direct recordings of near-death experiences.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Researcher Kenneth Ring has tried to quantify near-death experiences with a Weighted Core Experience Index.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Modern efforts have led to a new theory of near-death experiences, which goes like so...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}If an individual is dying, yet denies that fact...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They'll eventually come to realize that death is a reality they must accept, and become upset.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}As a result, they'll try to do anything they can to extend their life.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But the futility of this leads to depression.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}In the end, they accept their fate.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Although we may initially deny death, we'll accept it in the end. That's just how we humans are.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_185_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}...Is everyone listening? It's not every day that you hear a lesson like this! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How about a test to see if you were paying attention?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and your memories of life at school will disappear! "Atah gibor le-olam, Adonai"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_186_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}What do we call the feeling of joy that comes from a near-death experience?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_188_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14818 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Good work... That is correct. You'll have a happy school life.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_189_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong. You won't be seeing yourself in the yearbook...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_190_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Some people say a near-death experience awakens one's higher consciousness.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Whether or not that's true, there is an observably lower fear of death found in those who have endured one.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Perhaps this is because near-death experiences are surprisingly free of pain and suffering.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But this is not to say I'd want any of you to seek one out. Use the time you have here wisely, okay?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, that's all for today. Class is dismissed.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_191_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15809 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Happy new year, everyone. Winter break has come and gone.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}No more boozing— Oh, you guys are underage. Wow, I feel sorry for you...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_192_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But since it's a new year, here's a relevant question for you all.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_193_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Do you know about the monk named Ikkyu? He's the one who's famous enough that they made a show based on his life.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Well, this is what he said about New Year's...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"The new year is the index of a journey to a certain place." Where is this "certain place" he's referring to?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_195_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"Our New Year's decorations are but milestone markers along the path to the underworld."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_196_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 15843 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Womp-womp! The correct answer is "the underworld."[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}"Our New Year's decorations are but milestone markers along the path to the underworld."[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_197_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Ikkyu was one eccentric old man.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}During the new year celebration, he carried around a staff with a skull on top of it...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}And he'd say stuff like, "Death is coming closer," to random passersby and whatnot...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, if you want to learn more about old-timey stuff like this, you can go ask Ekoda— I mean, Mr. Ekoda about it.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1581 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Now, please open your textbooks.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_198_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14801 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Let's get started, shall we? "Xilka, Xilka, Besa, Besa"...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There's been an awful lot going on in the world lately...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}But you shouldn't let it get to you.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Many people are overly susceptible to the effects of the occult.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The worst thing a magician can do is lose control.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Always do what must be done, and you'll be fine.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, today's lecture is on the magic of ancient Greece.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Greece is known as the birthplace of Western philosophy and rationality, but...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The art of the curse advanced right alongside these lofty notions.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}While Greece was home to the great philosophers like Socrates, Thales, and Plato...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It was also the home to legendary enchantresses.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}For example, there was Medea, the passionate sorceress of Colchis.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}She was a witch who didn't hesitate to kill her brother or even her own children.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Then there was Circe, daughter of Helios, who was an expert in both poison and medicine.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Without Hermes's help, the legendary Odysseus would have been turned into a pig by Circe.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}These may have only been myths, but by reading between the lines, you can see ancient Greece's views on magic.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Greek magic began to take shape around the 8th century BC.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}It was around the same time that the "poleis," or city-states, were developing.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Like many ancient civilizations, they worshiped gods for protection.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}There were temples that priestesses tended—these women would double as oracles.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Gradually, however, these ceremonies were performed in secret rather than in public.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}The reason for this was the people's desire to monopolize the power of the gods.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}They copied the barbaric practices of legend: sacrifices, drinking, orgiastic festivals...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Dangerous acts such as these became widespread.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}These were the gods they invoked when performing the rituals.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_199_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Are you all paying attention? Don't cross the River Styx, now! Eeeheehee...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 1 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}How about I toss out a question for you?[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Get it wrong and your aura will shift! "Xilka, Xilka, Besa, Besa"...[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_200_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}I've mentioned two Greek sorceresses. One was Medea. Who was the other?[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_202_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14817 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Very good! Your aura is as clear as a mountain spring.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_203_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 3 1 3 0 -1 14820 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Wrong! Your aura is now muddy and slightly smelly.[n]{f 1 1}

Dialog.name: MSG_204_0_0
Dialog.Page: {f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Anyway, back to the lecture. It was that secrecy which turned priestesses into sorceresses.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Teachings originally meant to bring salvation to the body and soul fell into mere debauchery...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Which lured people down the hideous path of immoral practices.[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}Necrophilia, using babies as potion or spell ingredients...[n]{f 1 1}
{f 0 5 254}{f 2 1}{f 4 5 1081 0 -1 -1 0 0}{f 5 21 0 100}This was the birth of the abominable "dark arts"...[n]{f 1 1}
